items.q: When did Nazi Germany move to a total war economy?
items.correct: Only after Stalingrad in 1943, under Speer; before that Hitler had avoided full mobilisation to maintain civilian morale and consumer goods.
items.misconception: Germany was on a total war footing from 1939 onwards.
items.random_wrong: Germany never moved to a war economy because Hitler thought he would win quickly.
items.why: Germany famously delayed full mobilisation, expecting short wars. Students assume dictatorship meant immediate total war; in fact Hitler feared domestic unrest.

items.q: Why did Britain restrict Commonwealth immigration from 1962?
items.correct: Rising public hostility, political pressure, and economic concerns led to the 1962 Commonwealth Immigrants Act introducing voucher controls, followed by tighter restrictions in 1968 and 1971.
items.misconception: Britain restricted Commonwealth immigration because there was no work available.
items.random_wrong: Britain restricted immigration because the Commonwealth ceased to exist in 1962.
items.why: There was labour demand but political backlash drove restriction. Students assume economics drives policy; in fact race politics and public opinion were decisive.

items.q: Why did Henry VIII break with Rome?
items.correct: Primarily for an annulment from Catherine of Aragon, but also to seize Church wealth and assert royal supremacy — not for Protestant theological reasons.
items.misconception: Because he wanted England to become Protestant.
items.random_wrong: Because the Pope excommunicated him for marrying Anne Boleyn.
items.why: Henry remained doctrinally Catholic to his death. The break was political and dynastic; Protestantism only took hold in England under Edward VI and Elizabeth I.

items.q: What does the 'M' in MAIN stand for?
items.why: Correct answer: Militarism. “Money” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
It's Franz Ferdinand. “Kaiser Wilhelm” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: On what day and month of 1918 did the Armistice begin (day of the month)?
items.why: Exact answer: 11. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
